references.title_en: Training Verifiers to Solve Math Word Problems
references.title_zh: 训练验证器以解决数学应用题
references.authors_or_org: Karl Cobbe, Vineet Kosaraju, et al. (OpenAI)
references.summary_zh: 提出 GSM8K 数据集,并确立'生成器+验证器'范式:测试时采样多份候选解,用单独训练的验证器(reward model)给候选打分并选最高分。结论是验证比单纯微调更省数据、扩展性更好——这是 best-of-N、reward model 与'生成易、验证更可靠'思想的源头,直接支撑 loop 工程里用一个独立打分器来挑选/排序 agent 多次尝试的设计。
references.summary_en: Introduces GSM8K and establishes the generator+verifier paradigm: sample many candidate solutions at test time, then use a separately trained verifier (reward model) to score and select the top one. It shows verification scales better with data than finetuning — the origin of best-of-N, reward models, and the 'generate cheaply, verify reliably' idea, directly grounding loop designs that use an independent scorer to rank/select an agent's repeated attempts.
references.reliability_notes: Primary OpenAI arXiv paper (2021); seminal source for verifier/reward-model methodology.

references.title_en: RLEF: Grounding Code LLMs in Execution Feedback with Reinforcement Learning
references.title_zh: RLEF:用强化学习把代码大模型锚定在执行反馈上
references.summary_zh: 端到端 RL 方法,教 Llama 3.1 在多轮代码合成中真正利用执行反馈:每轮在公开测试上评估,失败则把反馈插回对话,通过/到轮次上限后用私有测试结果作为奖励,以 PPO 更新。在 CodeContests 上达 SOTA 并把所需采样量降低一个数量级,且泛化到 HumanEval+/MBPP+。这是'单元测试当奖励、执行反馈进循环'范式的一手实证,直接示范如何用可执行验证信号驱动 agent 的 plan-act-verify-iterate 闭环。
references.summary_en: An end-to-end RL method that teaches Llama 3.1 to actually use execution feedback in multi-turn code synthesis: each turn is graded on public tests, failures are fed back into the conversation, and on pass/turn-limit the private-test result is the reward, updated via PPO. It reaches SOTA on CodeContests while cutting required samples by an order of magnitude and generalizes to HumanEval+/MBPP+. A first-hand demonstration of the unit-test-as-reward / execution-feedback-in-the-loop paradigm, showing how an executable verify signal drives an agent's plan-act-verify-iterate closure.
references.reliability_notes: Primary Meta FAIR arXiv paper; accepted ICML 2025; strong empirical evidence for execution-feedback loops.

references.title_en: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
references.title_zh: SWE-bench: 大语言模型能否解决真实世界的 GitHub Issue?
references.authors_or_org: Carlos E. Jimenez, John Yang, et al. (Princeton, University of Chicago)
references.summary_zh: 提出 SWE-bench:从 12 个 Python 仓库的真实 GitHub issue+PR 构造的 2,294 道任务,模型须读懂跨文件代码并提交 patch,正确性由仓库自带的单元测试(FAIL_TO_PASS / PASS_TO_PASS)裁定。这把'通过预留测试'确立为编码 agent 的客观、可执行的终止/验证信号,是 loop 工程中'用测试当裁判'范式的基准源头;论文同时记录了当时最强模型仅解 1.96% 的难度基线。
references.summary_en: Introduces SWE-bench: 2,294 tasks built from real GitHub issues+PRs across 12 Python repos, where a model must edit a codebase and is graded by the repo's own unit tests (FAIL_TO_PASS / PASS_TO_PASS). It establishes 'passing held-out tests' as the objective, executable verify/stop signal for coding agents — the canonical benchmark grounding the unit-test-as-reward paradigm — and documents that the strongest model of the time solved only 1.96%.
references.reliability_notes: Primary arXiv paper, ICLR 2024; the foundational and most-cited coding-agent benchmark.

references.title_en: LLMs-as-Judges: A Comprehensive Survey on LLM-based Evaluation Methods
references.title_zh: LLMs-as-Judges:基于大语言模型的评估方法综述
references.authors_or_org: Haitao Li, Qian Dong, et al.
references.summary_zh: 对 LLM-as-judge 做系统综述:统一定义裁判的功能形态(打分/成对比较/逐条评判),归纳如何构建评估体系、缓解偏差(位置、长度、自偏好)、衡量裁判可靠性,并梳理应用领域。对 loop 工程是一份'如何把 LLM 裁判工程化为可靠验证器'的方法论清单——在没有单元测试的开放式任务里,这决定了验证/终止信号是否可信。
references.summary_en: A systematic survey of LLM-as-judge: it unifies the functional forms (pointwise scoring, pairwise comparison, listwise judging), catalogs how to construct evaluation systems, mitigate biases (position, length, self-preference), and measure judge reliability across application domains. For loop engineering it is a methodology checklist for engineering an LLM judge into a trustworthy verifier — decisive for open-ended tasks lacking unit tests where the verify/stop signal must still be reliable.
references.reliability_notes: Comprehensive 2024 arXiv survey; secondary-synthesis but broad and well-cited; good methodology map.

references.authors_or_org: Hunter Lightman, Vineet Kosaraju, Karl Cobbe, et al. (OpenAI)
references.summary_zh: 对比过程监督(对每个中间推理步给反馈)与结果监督(只对最终答案给反馈),发现过程奖励模型(PRM)显著更优,在 MATH 子集上解出 78%,并开源 80 万条步骤级标注 PRM800K。对 loop 工程的意义:验证不必是终点处的二元通过/失败,可以是逐步的密集反馈信号——为'按步骤验证、及早纠偏'的 agent 循环提供了奖励建模依据。
references.summary_en: Compares process supervision (feedback on each intermediate reasoning step) vs outcome supervision (feedback only on the final answer), finding process reward models (PRMs) markedly better — solving 78% of a MATH subset — and releases PRM800K, 800K step-level labels. For loop engineering: verification need not be a terminal binary pass/fail; it can be a dense, per-step signal, providing the reward-modeling basis for agent loops that verify step-by-step and correct course early.
references.reliability_notes: Primary OpenAI arXiv paper (2023); foundational for process- vs outcome-reward and PRMs.

references.summary_zh: OpenAI 官方发布的人工校验子集:93 名资深开发者审查原始 SWE-bench,剔除 68.3% 测试桩失效、描述不清或依赖环境的任务,保留 500 道'干净'任务,并按人类预估修复时长打难度标签(易/中/难/很难)。它直指 loop 验证的核心隐患——评测标准本身被污染(测试过严或题面欠规约会让 agent 即使'对了'也判错),因此设计可信终止信号时必须先确保验证集本身有效。
references.summary_en: OpenAI's official human-validated subset: 93 experienced developers audited the original SWE-bench, dropped 68.3% of tasks for broken test harnesses, underspecified statements, or environment dependencies, and kept 500 'clean' tasks labeled by estimated human fix-time. It directly addresses a core loop-verification hazard — that the grading signal itself can be corrupted (overly specific tests / vague specs mis-judge a correct agent) — so a trustworthy stop signal first requires a valid verifier.
references.reliability_notes: First-party OpenAI engineering page; canonical URL rechecked on 2026-06-25.

references.title_en: Why Are AI Agent-Involved Pull Requests (Fix-Related) Remain Unmerged?
references.title_zh: 为什么 AI Agent 参与的修复 PR 仍未合并？
references.summary_zh: 分析 Codex、Copilot、Devin、Cursor、Claude Code 等 agent 生成的 8,106 个修复类 PR：65.0% 合并、26.1% 关闭未合并、8.9% 仍打开；未合并常与测试失败、修复不完整和流程/时机问题相关。说明 loop 验证不能只停在 agent 自述完成，必须覆盖 CI、评审和集成结果。
references.summary_en: Analyzes 8,106 fix-related PRs authored by Codex, Copilot, Devin, Cursor, Claude Code, and related agents: 65.0% merged, 26.1% closed without merge, and 8.9% still open; non-merge outcomes often involve failing tests, incomplete fixes, and workflow/timing issues. Shows loop verification must include CI, review, and integration outcomes rather than the agent's done claim.
references.reliability_notes: February 2026 arXiv preprint based on mined PR data; useful empirical signal, but labels and agent attribution may be noisy.

references.title_en: SWE-EVO: Benchmarking Coding Agents in Long-Horizon Software Evolution Scenarios
references.title_zh: SWE-EVO：长时域软件演进场景中的编码 Agent 基准
references.summary_zh: 用 OpenHands 与 SWE-agent 两类 agent 框架、18 个模型评估长期软件演进任务；报告最强模型在 SWE-EVO 上只解 25%，而同系模型在 SWE-bench Verified 上达 72.80%。提醒 loop KB 不应把短修复 benchmark 的成功外推到长程演进。
references.summary_en: Evaluates long-horizon software-evolution tasks with OpenHands and SWE-agent across 18 models; the best model resolves only 25% on SWE-EVO versus 72.80% on SWE-bench Verified. Warns against extrapolating short-fix benchmark success to long-horizon evolution.
references.reliability_notes: Recent arXiv preprint; strong cautionary signal, but benchmark and model versions should be rechecked before quantitative reporting.

references.title_en: Mind the Gap: Examining the Self-Improvement Capabilities of Large Language Models
references.title_zh: 注意差距:考察大语言模型的自我改进能力
references.authors_or_org: Yuda Song, Hanlin Zhang, Carson Eisenach, Sham Kakade, Dean Foster, Udaya Ghai
references.summary_zh: 提出并形式化'生成-验证差距'(generation-verification gap):模型挑出正确解的能力高于其独立生成正确解的能力,这一正差距是自我改进得以成立的根本量,且随预训练算力单调增大。对 loop 工程的核心启示:agent 的'迭代-自评-再迭代'循环只在验证能力强于生成能力时才有正收益——这直接决定了 self-refine 类循环该不该继续迭代、何时该停。
references.summary_en: Formalizes the generation-verification (GV) gap — a model's ability to pick the correct solution exceeds its ability to generate one independently — and shows this positive gap is the quantity governing whether self-improvement works, scaling monotonically with pretraining compute. Core implication for loop engineering: an agent's iterate-self-evaluate-iterate loop only yields net gains when verification outpaces generation — directly dictating whether a self-refine loop should keep iterating and when to stop.
references.reliability_notes: Primary arXiv paper, ICLR 2025; rigorous theoretical framing of the generator-verifier gap.

references.title_en: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
references.title_zh: 用 MT-Bench 与 Chatbot Arena 检验 LLM-as-a-Judge
references.authors_or_org: Lianmin Zheng, Wei-Lin Chiang, et al. (LMSYS / UC Berkeley)
references.summary_zh: 系统检验'用强 LLM 当裁判'的可行性:GPT-4 等强裁判与人类偏好的一致率超 80%,与人类彼此间一致率相当,从而把 LLM-as-judge 确立为可扩展、可解释、可替代昂贵人工评估的方法;同时定量揭示位置偏置、冗长偏置、自我偏好等系统性偏差。对 loop 工程意味着:当任务没有可执行测试时,LLM 裁判可充当验证/终止信号,但必须针对其已知偏差做缓解。
references.summary_en: Systematically validates using a strong LLM as a judge: GPT-4-class judges agree with human preferences at over 80% — the same level as humans agree with each other — establishing LLM-as-judge as a scalable, explainable substitute for expensive human eval, while quantifying systematic biases (position, verbosity, self-preference). For loop engineering: where executable tests don't exist, an LLM judge can serve as the verify/stop signal, but its known biases must be mitigated.
references.reliability_notes: Primary arXiv paper, NeurIPS 2023; the canonical LLM-as-judge reference.
